query: New Caledonia July 2025 autonomy agreement text Bougival Agreement Elysée-Oudinot Accord key provisions French Parliament bill status 2026
a_issues: Content A lacks the primary text and official parliamentary dossier, so key provisions must be inferred from secondary sources. One result (Wikipedia) is less authoritative. The Devpolicy snippet appears inaccurate on timing/status, saying the Senate adopted the constitutional legislation in February 2026 and that Assembly debate was scheduled for April, whereas the anchor shows Senate first reading/adoption tied to the bill deposited 14 October 2025 and the Assemblée nationale rejected it on 25 February 2026. A also includes some interpretive language ('failed constitutional reform', 'state/nationality') from commentary rather than official text.
b_issues: Content B also lacks the official Bougival and Elysée-Oudinot texts and the official French parliamentary dossier. It provides no direct official bill-status source for 2026, only journalistic mentions that Parliament aimed to translate the accords into law. It is therefore much weaker on the 'French Parliament bill status 2026' part of the query. Some snippets are partial and potentially ambiguous: Islands Business says 'French parliament aims...' without outcome; PINA discusses implications but not final legislative status; JURIST and RSIS are secondary summaries only. No result in B clearly states the National Assembly rejection in 2026.
reasoning: A better enables a correct answer because it contains a result explicitly stating the 2026 parliamentary outcome: the RFI snippet says French MPs blocked the reform plan and that the National Assembly voted 190 to 107 to reject the bill, which directly addresses the bill status. A also contains multiple sources summarizing key provisions of the Bougival accord, including creation of a 'State of New Caledonia' within the French Republic and transfer of foreign-relations powers, plus a follow-on January 2026 Elysée-Oudinot agreement. While these are secondary and one result has timeline inaccuracies, the set still gives an LLM enough to answer the main question with the crucial 2026 status. B has some useful secondary material on Bougival/Elysée-Oudinot provisions, but it lacks a clear source for the actual 2026 parliamentary disposition and therefore is less capable of supporting a correct complete answer.
